title: Sehra Akram

infobox.value: |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | Women's [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") | | | | Representing  [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan") | | | | [South Asian Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_South_Asian_Games "Badminton at the South Asian Games") | | | | Bronze medal – third place | [2019 Kathmandu-Pokhara](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2019_South_Asian_Games "Badminton at the 2019 South Asian Games") | Women's team | |
(born: 1 October 1995)[[2]](#cite_note-2) is a badminton player from [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan").

At the 57th National Badminton Championship held in Lahore, Pakistan, Akram competed in singles and women's doubles. In singles, she reached the semi-finals where she was beaten by her teammate, [Mahoor Shahzad](/wiki/Mahoor_Shahzad "Mahoor Shahzad"),[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3) the eventual champion.[[4]](#cite_note-4) In doubles she paired with [Huma Javeed](/wiki/Huma_Javeed "Huma Javeed") and was beaten by Mahoor Shahzad (Wapda) and [Palwasha Bashir](/wiki/Palwasha_Bashir "Palwasha Bashir") ([NBP](/wiki/National_Bank_of_Pakistan "National Bank of Pakistan")) in 2 sets (21-9 and 21-8).[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3)

["Mahoor, Murad grab national badminton singles titles"](https://nation.com.pk/01-Feb-2020/mahoor-murad-grab-national-badminton-singles-titles). *The Nation*. 1 February 2020. Retrieved 16 November 2020.

**Sehra Akram** (born: 1 October 1995)[[2]](#cite_note-2) is a badminton player from [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan").

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sehra_Akram&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

### National

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sehra_Akram&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: National")]

Akram represents [WAPDA](/wiki/Water_%26_Power_Development_Authority "Water & Power Development Authority") in domestic competitions including National Championships[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3) and National Games.

**2020**  
At the 57th National Badminton Championship held in Lahore, Pakistan, Akram competed in singles and women's doubles. In singles, she reached the semi-finals where she was beaten by her teammate, [Mahoor Shahzad](/wiki/Mahoor_Shahzad "Mahoor Shahzad"),[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3) the eventual champion.[[4]](#cite_note-4) In doubles she paired with [Huma Javeed](/wiki/Huma_Javeed "Huma Javeed") and was beaten by Mahoor Shahzad (Wapda) and [Palwasha Bashir](/wiki/Palwasha_Bashir "Palwasha Bashir") ([NBP](/wiki/National_Bank_of_Pakistan "National Bank of Pakistan")) in 2 sets (21-9 and 21-8).[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3)

### International

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sehra_Akram&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: International")]

Akram was part of the six member women's team which competed at the [2019 South Asian Games](/wiki/2019_South_Asian_Games "2019 South Asian Games") held in [Kathmandu](/wiki/Kathmandu "Kathmandu"), [Nepal](/wiki/Nepal "Nepal").[[5]](#cite_note-5)
